headline: BIS Final Decision & Order: Hans De Geetere and Hasa Invest (Belgium) — FR Doc. 2026-10682
what_changed: A Final Decision and Order has been published by the U.S. Department of Commerce Bureau of Industry and Security concerning Hans De Geetere and Knokke-Heist Support Corporation Management a/k/a Hasa Invest, both located in Knokke-Heist, Belgium [FR Doc. 2026-10682, Title]. The specific terms, findings, penalties, and denial order provisions of the Final Decision and Order are not stated in source [FR Doc. 2026-10682, Title].
who_is_affected: Hans De Geetere, an individual located at Paul Parmentierlaan 121 and Nyckeestraat 4, 8300 Knokke-Heist, Belgium, is a named respondent in this proceeding [FR Doc. 2026-10682, Title]. Knokke-Heist Support Corporation Management, also known as Hasa Invest, located at Paul Parmentierlaan 121 and Nyckeestraat 4, 8300 Knokke-Heist, Belgium, is a named respondent in this proceeding [FR Doc. 2026-10682, Title]. The scope of any denial order or civil penalty affecting third parties is not stated in source [FR Doc. 2026-10682, Title].
licence_posture: Not stated in source [FR Doc. 2026-10682, Title].
relevance_eu_exporters: The named respondents are Belgian persons and entities. Any denial order issued under this Final Decision and Order would restrict U.S.-origin items from being exported, reexported, or transferred to the named parties; however, the specific denial order terms, including any foreign direct product rule application or de minimis provisions, are not stated in source.
action_items: Obtain and review the full text of FR Doc. 2026-10682 to determine the specific terms of the Final Decision and Order, including any denial order scope and duration.
Screen Hans De Geetere and Knokke-Heist Support Corporation Management a/k/a Hasa Invest against BIS Denied Persons List and other restricted party lists.
Assess whether any existing or pending transactions involve the named Belgian parties and apply appropriate holds pending full text review.

Hans De Geetere, Nyckeestraat 4, 8300 Knokke-Heist, Belgium

materiality: Substantive for counterparties of the named persons: a final decision and order against Hans De Geetere and Hasa Invest (Knokke-Heist, Belgium) [FR Doc. 2026-10682, Order]. Enforcement outcome specific to the named parties; no change to controls of general application.

headline: Aviastar-TU (Moscow) TDO Renewed — Export Privileges Temporarily Denied
what_changed: An Order Renewing Temporary Denial of Export Privileges (TDO) has been published against Aviastar-TU, 5 b. 7 Leningradsky prospect g. Moskva, 125040, Moscow, Russia [FR Doc. 2026-06161, Title].
who_is_affected: Aviastar-TU, 5 b. 7 Leningradsky prospect g. Moskva, 125040, Moscow, Russia is the named subject of the renewed TDO [FR Doc. 2026-06161, Title].
instruments: Order Renewing Temporary Denial of Export Privileges
licence_posture: Temporary Denial of Export Privileges renewed against Aviastar-TU; specific denial terms, duration, and applicable EAR provisions are not stated in source [FR Doc. 2026-06161, Title].
action_items: Screen Aviastar-TU against current TDO list before processing any export, reexport, or transfer involving U.S.-origin items.
Assess whether any EU-origin transactions involve U.S.-content subject to foreign direct product rule or de minimis provisions — specific thresholds not stated in source.
Monitor Federal Register for full text of FR Doc. 2026-06161 to obtain denial order terms and duration.
materiality: Substantive for counterparties of the named entity: renewal of a temporary denial order continues the suspension of U.S. export privileges for Aviastar-TU (Moscow) [FR Doc. 2026-06161, Order]. No change to any control list or licensing policy of general application.

headline: BIS extends ECCN 3A090.a approved IC designer application deadline from April 13, 2026 to December 31, 2026
what_changed: BIS has amended Note 1 to ECCN 3A090.a in paragraphs a.(2) and (3) by removing the date 'April 13, 2026' wherever it occurs and replacing it with 'December 31, 2026' [FR Doc. 2026-06851, Supplement No. 1 to Part 774, ECCN 3A090 amendment]. The triggering date for authorized IC designer status and the submission deadline for applications to become an approved IC designer are both extended by approximately eight months [FR Doc. 2026-06851, SUMMARY]. Applicants who submit by December 31, 2026 may be considered authorized IC designers for 180 days after that date [FR Doc. 2026-06851, DATES]. BIS estimates the extension will result in approximately 20 additional approved IC designers and an increase of 385 burden hours under OMB Control Number 0694-0137 [FR Doc. 2026-06851, Rulemaking Requirements §2]. BIS also estimates approximately 75 additional advisory opinions and 38 additional burden hours under OMB Control Number 0694-0088 [FR Doc. 2026-06851, Rulemaking Requirements §2].
who_is_affected: Front-end fabricators and OSAT companies seeking to export, reexport, or transfer (in-country) certain applicable advanced logic integrated circuits under ECCN 3A090.a are affected [FR Doc. 2026-06851, SUPPLEMENTARY INFORMATION]. Entities that have submitted or intend to submit requests to BIS to be listed as approved IC designers, subject to End-user Review Committee (ERC) approval, are affected [FR Doc. 2026-06851, SUPPLEMENTARY INFORMATION]. Entities seeking to qualify as authorized IC designers under the criteria in Note 1 to 3A090.a are affected [FR Doc. 2026-06851, SUPPLEMENTARY INFORMATION].
instruments: 15 CFR Part 774, Supplement No. 1 (Commerce Control List), ECCN 3A090.a, Note 1, paragraphs a.(2) and (3)
effective_dates: Extended application deadline (approved IC designer): December 31, 2026
Authorized IC designer status period: 180 days following December 31, 2026 for timely applicants
licence_posture: The rule modifies the date-based triggering mechanism within Note 1 to ECCN 3A090.a, which provides a means to overcome the presumption applicable to front-end fabricators and OSAT companies under 15 CFR 742.6(a)(6)(iii)(A) [FR Doc. 2026-06851, SUPPLEMENTARY INFORMATION]. Entities that apply by December 31, 2026 may be considered authorized IC designers for 180 days after that date [FR Doc. 2026-06851, DATES]. The source does not state a general licence posture change beyond the date extension described [FR Doc. 2026-06851, SUPPLEMENTARY INFORMATION].
action_items: EU-based IC designers or entities involved in the supply chain for ECCN 3A090.a items who seek approved IC designer status should submit applications to BIS by December 31, 2026.
Review Note 1 to ECCN 3A090.a paragraphs a.(2) and (3) to confirm updated date references in compliance procedures.
Assess whether front-end fabricator or OSAT operations involving ECCN 3A090.a items require reliance on the approved or authorized IC designer pathway and update timelines accordingly.

citations.pinpoint: FR Doc. 2026-06851, Supplement No. 1 to Part 774, ECCN 3A090 amendment

materiality: Substantive but narrow: extends a compliance window — the Note 1 to ECCN 3A090.a application deadline moves from April 13, 2026 to December 31, 2026; the scope of controlled items is unchanged [FR Doc. 2026-06851, Supplement No. 1 to Part 774, ECCN 3A090 amendment].

headline: OFAC SDN list: 27 additions (Iran, Cuba, Cyber) + 1,730 internal-ID modifications
what_changed: Twenty-seven entities and individuals were added to the Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list across three sanctions programmes [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e]. Additions under IRAN-EO13902 include LAVASANI, Ahmad Navai [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57949], CDM TRADING LIMITED [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57934], ABADI, Shokufeh Rostam [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57945], MOHSEN KHANDAN AND PARTNERS GENERAL PARTNERSHIP COMPANY [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57933], KHANDAN, Mohsen [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57942], KHANDAN, Ali Asghar [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57943], DARBANI, Mohammad [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57947], LAVASANI AND PARTNERS GENERAL PARTNERSHIP COMPANY [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57948], LAVASANI, Amir Navai [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57950], SARSHARI, Zahra [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57946], and MOHAMMAD DARBANI AND PARTNERS EXCHANGE GENERAL PARTNERSHIP COMPANY [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57944]. One individual, ANSARI, Ali, was added under programmes IRAN-EO13876, SDGT, IFSR, and IRGC [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57957]. SMART GLOBAL LIMITED was added under IRAN-EO13876 and SDGT [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57960]. Additions under CUBA-EO14404 include GRUPO EMPRESARIAL DEL COMERCIO EXTERIOR [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:58051], CORPORACION ANTILLANA EXPORTADORA SA [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:58056], COREYDAN S.A. [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:58057], GRUPO EMPRESARIAL DE TRANSPORTE MARITIMO PORTUARIO [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:58054], ENETEC S.A. [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:58052], ORGANIZACION SUPERIOR DE DIRECCION EMPRESARIAL CAUDAL S.A. [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:58053], ASSOCIATION OF COMBATANTS OF THE CUBAN REVOLUTION [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:58046], MINISTRY OF TOURISM OF CUBA [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:58059], RAPID RESPONSE BRIGADES [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:58058], and MILICIAS DE TROPAS TERRITORIALES [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:58055]. Two additions under CYBER4 are FIRST VPN SERVICE [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57975] and RASHEVSKYI, Dmytro [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57978]. Two further added entries are present in the full event record but were truncated from this digest [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e]. A total of 1,730 existing SDN entries were modified; the sole field changed in all modified records is 'ids', indicating internal identifier updates with no change to designated names, programmes, or other substantive fields [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e].
who_is_affected: US persons are prohibited from transacting with all newly added SDN entries [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e]. Non-US persons, including EU exporters, whose transactions involve US-origin goods, software, or technology, or who route payments through US financial institutions, face secondary exposure with respect to the newly designated parties [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e]. Entities in the Iranian financial and exchange sectors are directly implicated by the IRAN-EO13902 additions, including exchange partnership companies and trading entities [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57933, 57944, 57948]. Cuban state-linked commercial, transport, tourism, and paramilitary entities are implicated by the CUBA-EO14404 additions [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:58051, 58054, 58059, 58058, 58055]. Cybersecurity-sector counterparties are implicated by the CYBER4 additions [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:57975, 57978]. Counterparties of the 1,730 modified entries are not substantively affected, as only internal identifiers were updated [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e].
relevance_eu_exporters: Indirect only. The source does not cite foreign direct product rule, de minimis thresholds, or transshipment provisions. EU exporters transacting with newly added SDN parties through US-dollar clearing, US financial intermediaries, or goods/software/technology of US origin may face secondary sanctions exposure, but no such mechanism is explicitly stated in the source [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e].
action_items: Screen all counterparties, beneficial owners, and transaction parties against the 27 newly added SDN entries using entity_ids 57934, 57942, 57943, 57944, 57945, 57946, 57947, 57948, 57949, 57950, 57957, 57960, 57975, 57978, 58046, 58051, 58052, 58053, 58054, 58055, 58056, 58057, 58058, 58059, and the two truncated additions [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e].

Retrieve the full event record (evt-7f989122424ada54) to identify the two truncated 'added' entries not included in this digest [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e].

Update internal restricted-party screening databases to reflect the 'ids' field changes across 1,730 modified entries to maintain database integrity, noting no substantive designation changes are indicated [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e].

Assess existing and pipeline transactions involving Iranian exchange/trading entities, Cuban state commercial and transport entities, and cybersecurity-sector counterparties against the newly designated parties [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e].

Consult OFAC's published Federal Register notices for each programme (IRAN-EO13902, IRAN-EO13876, SDGT, IFSR, IRGC, CUBA-EO14404, CYBER4) to determine effective dates and any applicable general licences, as these are not stated in the source [CSL:Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) - Treasury Department:snap-7e70fdca5d98945e].
entries_affected: [2 truncated — see full event record evt-7f989122424ada54]
[1730 modified entries — ids field only; see snap-7e70fdca5d98945e]

headline: Corrigendum deletes the sub-193 nm light-source criterion from Annex I entry 3B501.f.1.a
what_changed: The corrigendum of 9 February 2026 to Delegated Regulation (EU) 2025/2003 replaces the text of entry 3B501.f.1.a, 'A light source wavelength shorter than 193 nm; or', with 'Not used;' [OJ L 2026/90092, entry 3B501.f.1.a]. 'Not used' is the Annex I placeholder for a deleted provision: the sub-193 nm light-source criterion no longer exists as a route into entry 3B501.f.1, whose remaining sub-points are unchanged [OJ L 2026/90092, entry 3B501.f.1.a].
who_is_affected: Exporters and holders of prior classifications of align-and-expose step-and-repeat or step-and-scan wafer-processing equipment assessed under entry 3B501.f.1 [CELEX 02021R0821-20251115, entry 3B501.f.1].
effective_dates: Correction applies as part of Regulation (EU) 2025/2003, in force since 15 November 2025 — 'the day following that of its publication in the Official Journal' [CELEX 32025R2003, Art. 2]
relevance_eu_exporters: Directly applicable: Annex I of Regulation (EU) 2021/821 governs EU exporters' classification obligations, and the corrected criterion gates entry 3B501.f.1 [OJ L 2026/90092, entry 3B501.f.1.a].
action_items: Revalidate any classification that relied on the deleted sub-193 nm light-source criterion of entry 3B501.f.1.a — including lithography equipment previously caught by it.
Record the Annex I version relied upon (02021R0821-20251115) in the classification register.

parameter_deltas.before: A light source wavelength shorter than 193 nm; or

reclassification_impact: Holders of prior classifications relying on entry 3B501.f.1.a should revalidate against the corrected entry text: the sub-193 nm light-source criterion no longer exists, and equipment caught solely by it falls outside 3B501.f.1.a.
materiality: Editorial de-duplication, assessed as non-substantive: the deleted criterion of entry 3B501.f.1.a is verbatim-identical to entry 3B001.f.1.a, which the corrigendum did not touch [CELEX 02021R0821-20251115, entry 3B001.f.1.a]. Equipment meeting the sub-193 nm light-source parameter remains controlled under entry 3B001.f.1 — no change to what requires authorisation [CELEX 02021R0821-20251115, entry 3B001.f.1].
